As the early years of childhood are not helpful to others, our earliest youth should be dedicated to God, so that we do not waste any of the remaining short time in life. Happy and wise are those who seek the Lord and prepare for usefulness at an early age, when others are pursuing sinful pleasures, forming bad habits, and making harmful connections. Who can express the pain prevented by early devotion, and its blessed effects? Diligent self-examination and watchfulness will convince us of the deceitfulness and wickedness of our own hearts, and the sinfulness of our lives. We are encouraged here to humble ourselves before God, and to seek him, as Josiah did. And believers are taught not to fear death, but to welcome it when it takes them away from the evil that is coming. Nothing hastens the ruin of a people, nor prepares them for it, more than their disregard of attempts made for their reformation. Do not be deceived; God cannot be mocked. The flow and direction of affections only changes at the command of Him who raises up those who are dead in trespasses and sins. We behold special beauty in the grace the Lord bestows on those who, in tender years, seek to know and love the Savior. Has Jesus, the Day-spring from on high, visited you? Can you trace your knowledge of this light and life, like Josiah, from your youth? Oh, the unspeakable happiness of becoming acquainted with Jesus from our earliest years!
